subject: Geography
title: Rivers as Borders
description: A fast breakdown of Rivers as Borders for builders.

hook.text: The Rio Grande separates the US and Mexico for 3,000 km—but the river keeps moving.

buildup.text: Rivers seem like natural dividing lines. They're visible, hard to cross, and easy to map.

discovery.text: But rivers flood, shift course, and sometimes jump to entirely new channels.

twist.text: In 1864, the Rio Grande shifted, leaving a piece of Mexico on the US side for over a century.

climax.text: River borders create endless legal disputes because the 'line' refuses to stay put.

quiz.question: Why are rivers problematic as borders?
options: They are too wide to see across
